title: Arshas / Anorectal Clinic Proforma
subtitle: Specialty Clinic — Shalya Tantra (Arshas / Bhagandara / Parikartika)

fields.content: 🌿 Shalya Tantra Chikitsa Sutra for Anorectal: Bhaishajya (medical) for Kaphaja Grade I–II Arshas; Ksharakarma (Sarava or Sutra) for Grades II–III and Bhagandara; Sastrakarma (surgery) for Grade III–IV and recurrent. Never start Ksharasutra without confirming fistula tract anatomy.

⚠ Red flags requiring urgent referral: Change in bowel habit + weight loss + rectal bleeding in >40 yrs = Colorectal cancer screen. Single unilateral polyp in adult = biopsy before treatment. Fistula in Crohn's = specialist management.

fields.label: Piles Degree (Arshas Grade)
options: Grade II (prolapse on straining, reduces spontaneously)
Grade III (prolapse on straining, manual reduction needed)

fields.label: Proctoscopy Findings
fields.placeholder: Position (3/7/11 o'clock), mucosal appearance, internal piles, polyp, mass…

fields.label: Ksharasutra Notes
fields.placeholder: Thread type used, complications, patient compliance, diet restrictions…

fields.label: Clinical Notes
fields.placeholder: Pre-op notes, Snehana / Swedana given, Virechana status…

steps.title: Patient Positioning & Preparation
items: Preferred: Left lateral (Sim's) position — standard for most anorectal exams
Knee-chest position — better view for high lesions
Lithotomy — for combined vaginal + anorectal examination (Prasuti)
Ensure adequate lighting; explain procedure to patient; ensure privacy

steps.title: Perianal Inspection (Bahya Pariksha)
items: Skin tags — sentinel pile at 6 o'clock (fissure), tags at 3/7/11 o'clock (piles)
External haemorrhoids — dark blue-red, painful if thrombosed
Fistulous external openings — discharge, granulation tissue
Anal fissure — longitudinal tear, usually posterior midline (6 o'clock)
Condylomata / warts, excoriation (perianal itching)

steps.title: Digital Rectal Examination (DRE)
items: Lubricate well; insert finger gently with patient breathing out
Sphincter tone — normal (can grip), lax (obstetric injury / neurological), hypertonic (fissure, anxiety)
Anterior wall — prostate (M), rectovaginal septum (F)
Mucosa — smooth vs nodular vs mass vs external compression
Blood on glove — colour (fresh red vs dark = higher lesion)

steps.title: Proctoscopy (Guda Shodhana Yantra Pariksha)
items: Lubricate scope; insert to 10–12 cm depth
Withdraw slowly while rotating — systematic view of all walls
Internal haemorrhoids — 3, 7, 11 o'clock positions (in lithotomy)
Mucosal appearance — normal pink vs inflamed vs polyp vs ulcer
Internal openings of fistula — probe gently with fistula probe if identified

steps.title: Ksharasutra Assessment (if thread in situ)
items: Check thread position — still cutting through tissue vs displaced
Measure thread change (cutting rate = distance cut / days since last change)
Granulation tissue quality — good (pink, healthy) vs slough / infection
Do NOT pull thread forcibly — confirm position before changing
Document thread number and estimated remaining distance to complete
